dahi-puri.name: Dahi Puri

ingredients.prep: soaked overnight, drained (Rancho Gordo desi chana, recommended)

instructions: Place chickpeas in a medium saucepan and pour in water to cover by 2"; season with salt. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to medium-low and simmer, stirring occasionally and adding more water as needed to keep covered, until tender and very creamy, 45–60 minutes. Drain.

Meanwhile, combine potatoes and turmeric in another medium saucepan and pour in water to cover by 2"; season with salt. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to medium-low and simmer, stirring occasionally, until potatoes are tender, 8–10 minutes. Drain.
Purée tamarind concentrate, date syrup, ginger, 2 Tbsp. chaat masala, 1 tsp. cumin, 1 tsp. red chili powder, and ¼ cup cold water in a blender until smooth. Season tamarind chutney with salt.
Mix yogurt, 2 Tbsp. chaat masala, remaining 2 tsp. cumin, and remaining 2 tsp. red chili powder in a small bowl to combine; season with salt.

Pour oil into a large pot to come 3" up sides and fit with thermometer. Heat over medium until thermometer registers 350°. Working in batches, slip 6–10 puris into oil and fry, turning with a slotted spoon, until golden and puffed, about 30 seconds. Transfer puris to a paper-towel-lined baking sheet.

Break open puri on one side and fill each one with 1 Tbsp. chickpeas and 1 Tbsp. potatoes. Transfer to a platter; drizzle with spiced yogurt, tamarind chutney, and Scallion Chutney. Scatter red onion, cilantro, mint, and sev over and sprinkle with more chaat masala.

dark-chocolate-cookies-and-delicious-rec.name: Dark Chocolate Cookies: And Delicious Recipe
dark-chocolate-cookies-and-delicious-rec.original_name: Galletas de chocolate negro: receta fácil y deliciosa

ingredients.item: 200 g de chocolate negro troceado o chips de chocolate negro

instructions: Gather your oven at 180-3 and place baking paper on a plate. Don't forget this step, because otherwise your cookies could stick.
In a big bowl, beat up butter, brown sugar and white sugar until the mixture is soft and spongy. If you have an electrical mixer, this is the time to use it.
Add the eggs one by one, beating well after each addition. He then incorporates his vanilla extract and keeps beating. That's where the mix starts to smell so good that you'll have to eat raw.
En otro bol, mezcla la harina, el cacao en polvo, el bicarbonato de sodio y la sal. Estos son los ingredientes secos que van a hacer que tus galletas tengan ese sabor a chocolate profundo.
Add the dry ingredients to the bowl of moist ingredients and mix them up till everything's well incorporated.
Add pieces of black chocolate and mix with a spatula.

Use a spoon to place portions of dough onto the plate, leaving space between each cookie because they will expand a little while baking. Put the plate into the oven and baker for 10-12 minutes or till the edges are firm but the centre still looks a little soft.
Take the cookies out of the oven and let them cool up on the plate for a few minutes before transferring them to a grid.

delicious-caramel-apple-poke-cake.name: Delicious Caramel Apple Poke Cake
instructions: Preheat oven to 149°C (150 degrees C). Grease a 9x13-inch baking pan.

Combine white sugar, oil, and eggs in a large bowl; beat with an electric mixer until smooth. Add apples, flour, 1 teaspoon vanilla extract, and cinnamon; beat until a thick batter forms. Spread batter in the prepared pan.
Bake in the preheated oven until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean, about 70 minutes. Poke holes in the hot cake with a fork.

Combine brown sugar, butter, heavy cream, and 1 teaspoon vanilla extract in a small saucepan over medium heat. Bring to a boil; cook for 2 1/2 minutes. Pour immediately over the warm cake. Let cake sit for 3 hours to soak up the glaze.

dilled-shrimp-rejer.name: Dilled Shrimp (rejer)
instructions: Bring water to a boil in a large pot over high heat. Add salt, sugar, and dill sprigs; stir until sugar has dissolved. Pour in shrimp and cook until the shells turn pink, and the meat is no longer translucent, 3 to 4 minutes. Strain the shrimp through a colander, discard dill sprigs, and chill until cold in the refrigerator, about 30 minutes.

Once shrimp have cooled, peel and devein them; discard the shells. Whisk together oil, vinegar, minced dill, salt, and pepper; toss with shrimp meat to coat. Cover, and chill overnight in the refrigerator; serve with sprigs of fresh dill.
